fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: California Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to California - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity california, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to california, speech-language pathologist compact california, california speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensers Board
board.mailing_address:
Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensers Board
2005 Evergreen Street, Suite 2100
Sacramento, CA 95815-3817

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in California. Requires a master's degree, completion of Required Professional Experience (RPE), and a passing Praxis score.

license_types.title: RPE Temporary License
license_types.description: Temporary license issued to SLP graduates completing their Required Professional Experience (RPE). Valid for 36 weeks full-time or 72 weeks part-time under supervision of a licensed SLP.

steps.title: Submit Online Application via BreEZe
steps.description: Complete and submit the Speech-Language Pathologist application form electronically through the BreEZe Online Services portal. This is the first step for all applicants, including those seeking licensure by endorsement.

steps.title: Provide Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts verifying a master's or doctorate degree in speech-language pathology (or its equivalent) to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Board.

steps.title: Submit Verification of Supervised Professional Experience (SPE)
steps.description: Ensure the Verification of Supervised Professional Experience form, completed by your supervisor(s), is submitted to the Board to confirm the completion of required clinical hours and supervised experience.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Speech-Language Pathology Praxis Examination (ETS SLP Praxis #5343) and ensure that your official scores are sent directly from ETS to the California Board.

steps.title: Complete the California Law and Ethics Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the California Law and Ethics Examination, which is a jurisprudence exam administered online by the Board once your application is deemed complete.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Complete a criminal background check by submitting your fingerprints. California residents must use Live Scan, while out-of-state applicants will submit fingerprint hard cards.

documents.detail: From a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.
Score of 162 or higher on test 5331. Must be dated within the last five years.
Signed by supervising licensed SLP confirming 36 weeks full-time or 72 weeks part-time.
Live Scan fingerprinting submitted to the Department of Justice and FBI.

telepractice_text: California permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid California SLP license to provide services to clients located in California, regardless of the practitioner's physical location. All telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person services.
provisional_text: California issues an RPE Temporary License for graduates completing their Required Professional Experience (RPE). This allows supervised practice for 36 weeks full-time or 72 weeks part-time. The RPE supervisor must hold a current California SLP license.
reciprocity_text: California offers alternative licensure pathways for out-of-state SLPs. Applicants holding a current ASHA CCC-SLP or a valid license in another state may qualify for expedited review. However, California does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. All out-of-state applicants must submit a full application with California-specific documentation.

faqs.question: How much does a California SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial licensure cost is $150. This includes a $35 RPE temporary license application fee and $115 for the permanent license application. Biennial renewal is $150.

faqs.question: Does California accept the ASHA CCC-SLP for licensure?
faqs.answer: Holding the ASHA CCC-SLP provides an alternative pathway to California SLP licensure. CCC-SLP holders may qualify for expedited review, but must still submit a full California application with all required documentation and fees.

faqs.question: Can I practice telepractice as an SLP in California?
faqs.answer: Yes. California permits licensed SLPs to provide telepractice services. You must hold a valid California SLP license to treat clients located in California, and all telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person treatment.

faqs.question: Does California have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in California. To work in California, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to California?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in California while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: California offers alternative licensure pathways for out-of-state SLPs. Applicants holding a current ASHA CCC-SLP or a valid license in another state may qualify for expedited review. However, California does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. All out-of-state applicants must submit a full application with California-specific documentation.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in California
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in California, applicants must use the BreEZe Online Services portal to submit their application and required documentation.

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the BreEZe Online Services website.
2. Click on 'New User? Register Here' to create a new BreEZe account.
3. Follow the prompts to enter your personal information and create a username and password.
4. Once registered, log in to BreEZe and select the appropriate application type for Speech-Language Pathologist.
